popularStory.description: Eleanor of Landcaster (Countess of Arundel) died four years prior to Earl Richard FitzAlan. In his will Richard requested to be buried "near to the tomb of Eleanor de Lancaster, my wife; and I desire …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel於1306年出生在英國素可阿盧德爾時，他的父親Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundel21歲，母親Alice de Warenne19歲。他於1321年2月9日與Isabel le Despenser在英國艾可哈維林-亞特-包維爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1345年登記服兵役。他於1339年在威爾士凱爾納方史爾被列為high sheriff of caernarfonshire。他去世於1376年1月24日在英國素可阿盧德爾 卡素享年70歲，埋葬在英國素可其斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel于1306年出生在英国素可阿盧德爾时，他的父亲Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundel21岁，母亲Alice de Warenne19岁。他于1321年2月9日与Isabel le Despenser在英国艾可哈維林-亞特-包維爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1345年登记服兵役。他于1339年在威尔士凱爾納方史爾被列为high sheriff of caernarfonshire。他去世于1376年1月24日在英国素可阿盧德爾 卡素享年70岁，埋葬在英国素可其斯特。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel was born in 1306, in Arundel, Sussex, England, his father, Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundel, was 21 and his mother, Alice de Warenne, was 19. He married Isabel le Despenser on 9 February 1321, in Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He registered for military service in 1345. In 1339, his occupation is listed as high sheriff of caernarfonshire in Caernarfonshire, Wales. He died on 24 January 1376, in Arundel Castle, Sussex, England, at the age of 70, and was buried in Chichester, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel est né en 1306, à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundel, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Alice de Warenne, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Isabel le Despenser le 9 février 1321, à Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1345. En 1339, sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff of caernarfonshire à Caernarfonshire, Galles. Il est décédé le 24 janvier 1376, à Arundel Castle, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Chichester, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel 1306 in Arundel, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundel 21 und seine Mutter Alice de Warenne 19. Er hat am 9. Februar 1321 in Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, England Isabel le Despenser geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er hat sich 1345 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1339 ist sein Beruf als high sheriff of caernarfonshire in Caernarfonshire, Wales aufgeführt. Er starb am 24. Januar 1376 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Arundel Castle, Sussex, England und wurde in Chichester, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel è nato il nel 1306 nel Arundel, Sussex, England, il suo padre, Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundel, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Alice de Warenne, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Isabel le Despenser il 9 febbraio 1321, nel Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1345. Nel 1339, la sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff of caernarfonshire nel Caernarfonshire, Galles. Morì il 24 gennaio 1376, nel Arundel Castle, Sussex, England, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Chichester, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundelが1306年にイギリススセックスアルンデルで生まれた時，父Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundelは21歳，母Alice de Warenneは19歳であった。1321年2月9日にイギリスエセックスヘイバーイング-アッテ-ボウエルでIsabel le Despenserと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1345年に兵役登録をした。1339年，ウェールズケエルナルフォンシュアイアでの職業はhigh sheriff of caernarfonshireと記録されている。彼は1376年1月24日にイギリススセックスアルンデル カストルで70歳で死去し，イギリススセックスチチェスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel(이)가 1306년 영국 수세스 아런들에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundel(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Warenne(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1321년 2월 9일 영국 에세스 하버링-엇-보워에서 Isabel le Despenser(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1345년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1339년, 그의 직업은 웨일스 카이른아르폰실르에서 high sheriff of caernarfonshire이었다. 그는 1376년 1월 24일 영국 수세스 아런들 카스틀에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 치체스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel nasceu em 1306, em Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundel, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Warenne, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel le Despenser em 9 de fevereiro de 1321, em Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1345. Em 1339, sua profissão aparece como high sheriff of caernarfonshire em Caernarfonshire, Gales. Ele faleceu em 24 de janeiro de 1376, em Arundel Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Chichester, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel родился в 1306 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundel было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Alice de Warenne, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel le Despenser 9 февраля 1321 г. в местности хавиринг-атте-боуир, эссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1345 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1339 годуработал по профессии high sheriff of caernarfonshire в местности каирнарфоншир, Уэльс. Он умер 24 января 1376 г. в местности арундель кастл, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности шичестир, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel nació en 1306, en Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund FitzAlan 9th Earl of Arundel, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Alice de Warenne, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Isabel le Despenser el 9 de febrero de 1321, en Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1345. En 1339, su ocupación registrada era high sheriff of caernarfonshire en Caernarfonshire, Gales. Murió el 24 de enero de 1376, en Arundel Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Chichester, Sussex, Inglaterra.
